page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance type belonging to the ecs.se1 family, configured with 8 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 8 physical cores with a dedicated CPU allocation and 1.0 thread per core. The processor includes a 33 MB L3 cache and supports AVX and AVX-512F instruction sets, alongside AES cryptography and FMA mathematical acceleration. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the ecs.se1.2xlarge demonstrates moderate performance characteristics. Its memory subsystem achieves average read and write bandwidths on large uncached blocks, though small-block cached read bandwidth falls into the bottom 25%. Computational benchmarks, including stress-ng and Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, consistently rank in the bottom 25%, reflecting its older processor architecture. Similarly, single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds are poor, placing in the bottom 10% of tested systems. However, the instance maintains average performance in Passmark CPU physics and extended instruction tests, as well as Redis SET operations.

With a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.se1.2xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high-performance compute capabilities. The high memory density per core makes it suitable for caching layers, lightweight database operations, and memory-bound applications. However, the weak single-core performance and lack of local NVMe storage present tradeoffs for I/O-heavy or compute-intensive tasks. This server is best utilized for memory-optimized workloads such as Redis caching, moderate web serving, and lightweight data processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM on an x86_64 architecture. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz, it provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results indicate average performance in large-block memory bandwidth and Redis SET operations, but weak single-core and multi-core CPU performance, placing it in the bottom 25% for computational benchmarks. This resource profile makes the ecs.se1.2xlarge qualitatively cost-effective for memory-bound applications that do not demand high-frequency compute power. It is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as caching layers, lightweight databases, and general-purpose memory-optimized applications.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.2xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, designed for memory-bound caching and database workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for caching and memory-bound workloads without high compute demands.
